---
title: "02 Main Concepts"
---

# 🎲 Random

- [landmark.am](https://landmark.am/)

![image.png](../00_random_image/02_ancient_churches_Armenian.jpg)


# 📚 Նյութը

- [📺 [06] Մոդելի գնահատում. overfitting ու cross validation](https://youtu.be/1j_xYLQrUAg), [🎞️ Սլայդեր](04_overfitting_cross_validation.pdf), [📝 Նշումներով](04_overfitting_cross_validation_notes.pdf)

📝 **Թեմայի վերաբերյալ հարցաշար (Google Form):** TBD

***

# 🏡 Տնային

## Project - Audit and rescue a rent model 🧀🧀🧀

A teammate trained a rent predictor on `House_Rent_Dataset.csv` and is ready to ship it: *"log-Rent R² = 0.83, average error under 0.3, basically done."* Their notebook is **`buggy_rent_model.ipynb`** (in this chapter's folder). You are the reviewer. It hides six methodology mistakes from this chapter (L04-L07). Most of them will *not* change the headline score - broken methodology can still print a great number - so you find them by reading the code against the rules, not by hunting for a value that looks off. Find them, prove them, fix them, and report the *honest* number.

**Setup.** Start from `buggy_rent_model.ipynb`. Same data as HW2: `../01_regression_intro/data/House_Rent_Dataset.csv` (target `Rent`). Deliver one reproducible notebook, seed `509`.

**Part 1 - Audit (find the bugs).** The starter hides **six** planted mistakes. For each, fill one row of an audit table:

| # | Where (cell) | Rule it breaks (cite L04-L07) | Why the reported number is a lie |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ... | ... | ... |

There is also one thing *missing* that you should add: what baseline tells you whether R² = 0.83 is even good?

**Part 2 - Fix.** Rebuild it correctly: split first, all preprocessing inside a `Pipeline`, CV for model selection, a sane log-scale search space, scaling before any penalty, and the test set touched exactly once.

**Part 3 - Honest model + model card.** Add a `DummyRegressor` floor. Pick and justify ONE headline metric (the target is wildly skewed - RMSE, MAE, MedAE, R², MAPE tell different stories). Use residual diagnostics (predicted-vs-actual, residuals-vs-fitted) to find *where* the model fails. Close with a short **model card**: final test metric(s) reported once, chosen config, known failure modes, and a 3-5 sentence conclusion.

::: {.callout-important}
Most of these bugs barely move the score - that *is* the point. A model can be riddled with methodology errors and still print 0.83. Only the leakage and the log-units reporting actually distort the number here; the rest you catch only by reading the code.
:::

**Bonus 🎁:** nested CV for an unbiased estimate; target-encode `Area Locality` *correctly* (inside CV folds) instead of dropping it; a P10-P90 prediction interval; a per-city error breakdown.


<a href="http://s01.flagcounter.com/more/1oO"><img src="https://s01.flagcounter.com/count2/1oO/bg_FFFFFF/txt_000000/border_CCCCCC/columns_2/maxflags_10/viewers_0/labels_0/pageviews_1/flags_0/percent_0/" alt="Flag Counter"></a>
